ChecklistFailedItems: API-05 All list endpoints support pagination (limit/offset or cursor): GET /api/v1/admin/templates returns an unbounded list with no pagination query parameters or pagination metadata; add validated paging inputs and bounded queries.

PERF-04 Response payloads are bounded — no unbounded queries returning all rows: template listing currently returns all tenant templates at once; enforce server-side page limits and default/max page size.

TEST-06A LLD coverage is verified — generated code is checked against approved Low-Level Design: implementation is not fully aligned with provided component spec flows (notably endpoint contract mismatch for document generation/review paths and a queued branding orphaned-file-cleanup job type without a corresponding worker handler); align routes/flows and add traceable tests.

CODE-02 No hardcoded values — magic numbers, strings, URLs, timeouts, and schedules are in constants or config: operational literals (job statuses/types/priorities, retry and timeout values, role/permission strings) are still embedded in service logic; centralize them in configuration/constants.

ChecklistFailedItems: API-05 List endpoints support pagination (limit/offset or cursor): `GET /api/v1/admin/templates` has no pagination query parameters or bounded page-size enforcement; add `limit`/`offset` (or cursor) handling with validated bounds in controller/service/repository.

PERF-04 Response payloads are bounded — no unbounded queries returning all rows: admin template listing currently returns the full tenant template set without pagination limits; enforce server-side paging and maximum page size.

SEC-02 Rate limiting is applied to authentication and public endpoints: `TenantRateLimitMiddleware` bypasses limiting when `X-Tenant-Id` is missing, so anonymous/auth flows such as `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` can avoid throttling; add an IP/client-based fallback limiter for requests without tenant context.

SEC-06 Dependencies are pinned and free of known critical CVEs: lockfiles and CI vulnerability-gate logic exist, but current dependency vulnerability status was not verifiable in this workspace (`dotnet` CLI unavailable); run `dotnet list AeonDocGen.slnx package --vulnerable --include-transitive --format json` in an executable environment and confirm zero critical CVEs.

ChecklistFailedItems: List endpoints support pagination (limit/offset or cursor) — add pagination parameters and enforce page-bounded query execution for template listing.
Response payloads are bounded — no unbounded queries returning all rows — apply default/max page size and return only bounded template results.

LLD coverage is verified — generated code is checked against the approved Low-Level Design to confirm all required modules, flows, interfaces, validations, and edge cases are implemented — align document generation/review endpoint contracts with the approved component spec paths and close the resulting traceability gap.
Sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) is not logged — stop logging Idempotency-Key values and redact token-like identifiers from service logs.

Alerts or notifications are triggered on repeated job failures — integrate JobFailureAlert escalation with an actual notification channel (for example webhook/email/incident system), not only DB persistence.

File path traversal is prevented in any file-serving or file-reading logic — harden BrandingStorageClient/DocumentStorageClient path canonicalization to reject rooted/drive-qualified inputs and enforce base-path containment checks.

  "isCompliant": false,
  "failedItems": [

    "LLD coverage is verified — generated code is checked against the approved Low-Level Design to confirm all required modules, flows, interfaces, validations, and edge cases are implemented — align document generation/review endpoint contracts with the approved component spec paths and close the resulting traceability gap.",
    "Sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) is not logged — stop logging Idempotency-Key values and redact token-like identifiers from service logs.",
    "Alerts or notifications are triggered on repeated job failures — integrate JobFailureAlert escalation with an actual notification channel (for example webhook/email/incident system), not only DB persistence.",
    "File path traversal is prevented in any file-serving or file-reading logic — harden BrandingStorageClient/DocumentStorageClient path canonicalization to reject rooted/drive-qualified inputs and enforce base-path containment checks."
  ],
  "summary": "Implementation is not fully checklist-compliant: core API and DB foundations are strong, but pagination/bounded-list behavior, LLD traceability alignment, sensitive log redaction, actionable failure alerting, and storage path hardening remain incomplete."
